dialogos.texto_en: Wow, it's even more lively than I imagined!\nLook at how many shops there are!
It's amazing, right?\nI was like 'woah' when I came here yesterday too.
Oh look, they're selling chinsuko!\nLet's get a pack later, big bro.
Sure.\nIs it for a souvenir?
Yeah, for my friends at school.\nI promised to bring back souvenirs from Okinawa.
We should probably start thinking about souvenirs too.\nIt's a hassle to buy them at the last minute.
I'm not sure what to get though.\nFood is always a safe choice, right?
Yes, local sweets are always a good option.
But, I was thinking of getting something\nother than snacks for Dad's souvenir.
I wonder what kind of souvenir would make him happy?
(I'm sure he would be grateful for anything\n from his daughters, but, that guy...)
What about a souvenir for our dad?
Something like sweets would be fine.\nHe said anything is okay,\nso we don't have to worry about it.
Okay, so what should we do?\nDo we split up and look around?
Sounds good.\nWe want to make the most of our limited time here.
Hmm, chinsuko is nice,\nbut there are so many famous things in Okinawa,\nright?
Raiha-chan,\nwould you like to look for souvenirs together?
Yes, please!\nThank you, Yotsuba-san.
Yay!\nA shopping date with Raiha-chan!
(I see, so shopping is a date.\n In that case, this is my chance to invite Nino\n and take the lead!)
Hey, Futaro%p-2;――
Nino!
Wh-wh-what?\nDon't suddenly shout like that.
Ah, sorry.\nI just wanted to look around the shops with you.
Huh...?
Ah, no, I mean, if you don't mind.\nIf there's somewhere you want to go%p-2;――
I don't mind!\nI was just surprised, that's all.
I see, that's good.
Let's set a meeting time and split up for now, then.
Okay, sounds like a plan.\nSee you guys later.
Alright, let's go, Nino.
Yeah!
